channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Joint Statement from OpenAI and Microsoft
item.description: The OpenAI story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via OpenAI Blog).

item.title: Trump orders federal agencies to drop Anthropic’s AI
item.description: Depending on your setup, Trump might save you time or headaches down the line (via The Verge AI).
item.author: Hayden Field, Richard Lawler

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: What’s actually at stake?
item.description: Not a fire drill, but Anthropic is the kind of thing that compounds if you ignore it (via TechCrunch AI).
item.author: Rebecca Bellan

item.title: Musk bashes OpenAI in deposition, saying ‘nobody committed suicide because of Grok’
item.description: If Musk pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via TechCrunch AI).
item.author: Sarah Perez

item.title: Agent Observability Powers Agent Evaluation
item.description: You can't validate improvements without systematic evaluation.
item.author: LangChain Accounts

item.title: OpenAI Fires an Employee for Prediction Market Insider Trading
item.description: The OpenAI story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via Wired AI).
item.author: Kate Knibbs

item.title: Google and the Massachusetts AI Hub are launching a new AI training initiative for the Commonwealth.
item.description: Google just dropped — worth a quick test if you're hitting limits with current tools (via Google AI Blog).

item.title: Google DeepMind Introduces Unified Latents (UL): A Machine Learning Framework that Jointly Regularizes Latents Using a Diffusion Prior and Decoder
item.description: Unified Latents (UL) going open lowers the bar for experimentation and custom deployments (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: Last Week in AI #336 - Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Anthropic vs Pentagon
item.description: If Week pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via Last Week in AI).
item.author: Last Week in AI

item.title: Block lays off 40% of workforce as it goes all-in on AI tools
item.description: Depending on your setup, Block might save you time or headaches down the line (via Ars Technica AI).
item.author: Peter Wells and Akila Quinio, Financial Times

item.title: Mixture of Experts (MoEs) in Transformers
item.description: The Mixture story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via Hugging Face Blog).

item.title: MySQL 9.6 Changes Foreign Key Constraints and Cascade Handling
item.description: Depending on your setup, MySQL might save you time or headaches down the line (via InfoQ).
item.author: Renato Losio

item.title: AI is rewiring how the world’s best Go players think
item.description: Depending on your setup, AI is rewiring how the world’s might save you time or headaches down the line (via MIT Technology Review AI).
item.author: Michelle Kim

item.title: After Orthogonality: Virtue-Ethical Agency and AI Alignment
item.description: Depending on your setup, Ethical Agency might save you time or headaches down the line (via The Gradient).
item.author: Peli Grietzer

item.title: Agent Economy Daily #12: MoonPay Gives AI Agents Their Own Wallets
item.description: Teams already working in this space should skim the Agent Economy Daily details (via Dev.to AI).
item.author: Neil Volner

item.title: AI Is Acing Math Exams Faster Than Scientists Write Them
item.description: Not a fire drill, but AI Is Acing Math Exams Faster Than is the kind of thing that compounds if you ignore it (via IEEE Spectrum AI).
item.author: Benjamin Skuse

item.title: A new way to express yourself: Gemini can now create music
item.description: If Gemini pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via Google DeepMind).
